key_insight: AA-ViT uses anatomy-, structure-, and frequency-aware transformers to synthesize contrast-enhanced brain MRI from non-contrast scans while better preserving lesion and tissue boundaries.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, this could reduce reliance on gadolinium contrast, shorten scan workflows, and improve access to post-contrast-like imaging when contrast agents are unsuitable.
method: Input pre-contrast MRI modalities: T1, T2, and FLAIR
Use a vision transformer guided by anatomical structure and frequency cues
Generate synthetic contrast-enhanced MRI for lesion visibility preservation
evidence_signal: On BraTS 2021, the model outperformed state-of-the-art methods on PSNR and SSIM and received a mean clinical rating of 3.94/5 from three neuroradiologists and one neurosurgeon across 19 cases.
watch_item: This is a synthesis model, not a replacement for true contrast imaging; performance, generalizability, and safety need broader external validation before clinical use.
paper_title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis
